channel.title: Christos Tsanikidis - CS, Math, Life
channel.description: Recent content on Christos Tsanikidis - CS, Math, Life

item.title: About
item.description: Dear visitor, my name is Christos Tsanikidis and I am a PhD Student at Columbia University, currently living in New York City in the US. I completed my Master’s degree at Columbia University. Before that I completed 5-Year Engineering studies in the school of ECE at NTUA in Athens. Currently I am doing research on Computer Networks, where I focus on providing new algorithms that preferably have some performance guarantees. More broadly I am interested in Algorithms, Stochastic Modeling, Optimization and Machine Learning.

item.title: Modelling and Simulating a Pandemic
item.description: Disclaimer: This is a rough model. The author is not an epidemiologist. The model was built based on his anecdotal experience. Warning:This is intended for large enough screens. You can’t see the whole enviornment using your phone. Adjust the Parameters below to explore the spread of the disease under different scenarios. Bear in Mind that some parameters might lead to poor performance depending on your device. Population Density Town Size Connections Radius Connectivity Chance Infection Chance Mean Days Sick Can you get Reinfected?

item.title: Learning by Generalizing and Exploring
item.description: When learning, the following principles can be very useful: generalizing what we learn, exploring the concept we learn, naming things. Generalizing-Exploring helps us learn more than what we are actually taught while naming help us recognize-apply what we learn in random occurances in our lives [1]. When we learn a new practical technique, the steps required to start incorporating it in our lives are: understand the technique/advice, make it a habit (the hard part).

item.title: Steps taken for the creation of a virtual football betting game
item.description: -- I have already dedicated a previous post on the simulation of the doubling method. In this post I will walk you through steps that can be taken for the creation of a virtual betting game. In particular we will examine a football betting game, similar to one currently found in the market. Design decisions Initially, before we develop the simulation, we can discuss two different possible methods to create such a game.

item.title: A functional approach for solving equations
item.description: Solving or interpreting equations is a fundamental topic, usually taught in young ages, when students are not very eager to capture the essence of the equations or do not know some more advanced concepts that can reinforce their understanding of them. When students are presented with an equation $x+4=13$, usually they are asked about the value of $x$. As a student I was shown a number of manipulations that I had to learn in isolation, making the task cumbersome initially but then mechanic.

item.title: A Gambler, enlightened - A simple simulation of the Doubling Method
item.description: When I’m having conversations with people about gambling, I come across massive misinformation and misunderstanding, and should I attempt to explain, the normal person, understandably, struggles to understand in a casual conversation that probabilities can guarantee your loss in long term with numerous fallacies [3] complicating that attempt. The problem is exacerbated when we consider games that intentionally convolute the determining mechanism, making it harder to infer that you are deemed (again in the long run) to lose your money.

item.title: Counting the number of elements in Sequences made simple
item.description: Counting the number of elements in a sequence of elements can often be confusing, and this is something that frequently comes up in mathematics, computer science or even programming (I won’t get deeper in the use cases in this post). Thankfully there is a simple method that I am using in such scenarios which makes these mini problems straightforward. Only basic mathematics are required. Examples First Let’s start in an unorthodox fashion, providing a few examples before the description.

item.title: Athens mini-markets Voronoi
item.description: The process followed was the following: Download data containing the addresses of 1404 mini markets in athens. Use google API to query the latitude and longitude of the addresses, given the information of the location (area and address). Convert latitude and longitude to cartesian coordinates, assuming linearity with small error due to the restricted range. Use google maps api to keep a white simplified map of Athens.

item.title: QuTy - M/M/1 web simulation
item.description: This is a web simulation of a M/M/1 queue and server for educational purposes. Axis/graph/queue are drawn with custom code. The system is stable. Check the web simulation Possible Extension Allow the user to create a Directed acyclic graph of queues-servers with possible inspection of the graph of each queue. Allow modification of parameters

item.title: Turkey to Greece missile range
item.description: It was announced in Greek media that Turkey achieved a new maximum range with modern missiles in its disposal. Immediately the question that I was curious about was, what targets in Greece can be hit by Turkey now? Before I begin, I want to make clear that this experiment was created for fun and that I am not that pessimistic about the relations of the two countries. Problem Hit Area if \(\mathcal{T}\) the set of points that are in Turkey.

item.title: Unified Terminal Experience, BACU
item.description: Motivation In this page I am going to demonstrate an idea of a unified terminal experience. To substantiate it, I created an application for SQL, using ncurses and python, but the concept is to apply the same principle in any hierarchical tree structure, aiding the development by an underlying library. The interaction we have each day with computers varies from application to application. This is also the case for terminal experience.

item.title: Blind Hunter
item.description: Suppose you are a hunter that doesn’t have information about the distance from the prey. For instance a blind dog with very precise smelling which can be mapped to a distance from the potential prey. How can this dog locate and eventually reach its prey? The algorithms are not documented yet, but a simulation is provided. If you are interested contact me for more information. Demo simulation parameters: \( V_{prey} \approx V_{predator} \) Prey changes direction with small probability Predator adopts the strategy we have assigned it (not explained here).

item.title: Heat Equation PDE Simulation
item.description: Heat equation The heat equation is a partial differential equation, that is, it involves both derivatives of the time and of spatial variables. $$\frac {\partial u} {\partial t} - \alpha \frac {\partial^2 u}{\partial x^2} = 0 $$ Where the parameter \(\alpha\) determines how much conductive the material is. This is the case for one variable, and thus one dimension. You can imagine that as a long but very thin wire. Of course in reality there is no such thing as 1 Dimension material, but from physicists and mostly engineers we know that this can be approximated when the long side is much bigger than the radius of the wire.

item.title: Hierarchies - Logarithmic counting for humans
item.description: Assumption: Rearrangement of objects for counting is possible. People are capable of processing and understanding a very specific range of patterns with ease. Likely a subset of those relevant to our past survivability. For instance we are unable to enumerate instantly small number of objects - for today’s requirements - and therefore we have to reduce the problem into maintanable chunks, appropriate for our capabilities. A way to reduce the problem is building hierarchies.

item.title: Mice Make Art!
item.description: Concept The concept is the following: Having N mice, deploy them randomly in a rectangular area. Each mouse targets exactly one other mouse. Every mouse is targeted by one mouse, forming a circle of references-targets. Phenomena Each mouse \(A\) is confined in the rectangular area: since it targets a mouse \(B\) that is within the rectangular area, it moves in this direction. Mouse \(B\) itself targets a mouse \(C\) which for the same reason is in the rectangular area.

item.title: Regex Experimenting Platform
item.description: This is an experimenting platform I’ve created for people who are learning regular expressions. Javascript Regular Expressions are used. The platform allows you to experiment by entering a regular expression, and then highlighting the matching parts. Match and replace is also available, with special regex replacing syntax available. You can either use the reload button or edit the text yourself in the platform. Experiment with the platform yourself Demonstration

item.title: Warcraft 3 Mod - Pimp my Peon
item.description: Pimp my Peon was the first contact I had with programming at the age of 15-16 and very influential to the later developed interests. Alternatively you might consider it as an early indication of an innate fascination for the art of “creation” using technology. It is documented here for historical reasons. It was a mod for Warcraft 3, like the famous Dota 1, which had begun as an open source project and was then “forked” by young me.
